scope_boundary: Planning scheduler-state probe only. The probe validates two deterministic synthetic scheduler traces and rejects seven expected-invalid controls for blocked-node dispatch, ready-without-context dispatch, failed-adequacy route selection, conflicting merge acceptance, replanning authority erasure, dependency-cycle acceptance, and hidden-cost ledger erasure. It does not execute a deployed planner, deployed scheduler, runtime replanning loop, decomposition-quality evaluator, context-demand predictor, route-quality evaluator, selected-tier adequacy evaluator, scheduler-optimality test, benchmark, model-quality evaluation, safety evaluation, or ASI system.
transition_reason: The Planning scheduler-state probe is useful because it makes ready, blocked, dispatchable, merged, repaired, and residualized scheduler states executable as finite Planning records while forcing cheap-route adequacy failure, conflicting assumptions, authority preservation, dependency acyclicity, and repair/human-review/residual costs to stay visible. It remains a deterministic synthetic record gate, not deployed scheduler evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed planner behavior, runtime scheduler behavior, decomposition quality, context-demand prediction, selected-tier adequacy, route quality, scheduler optimality, runtime replanning, live feedback handling, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Planning chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable scheduler trace
planner harness with real or externally replayed plan-node lifecycle states
dispatch trace showing ready, blocked, residualized, and dispatched nodes under realistic dependencies
decomposition-quality measurement over task objectives and constraints
scheduler-optimality or cost-quality comparison with visible residuals
runtime-replanning trace with authority and stop-condition preservation
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: repair, human-review, failed-attempt, or residual cost erasure is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: blocked-node dispatch, ready-without-context dispatch, failed-adequacy route selection, conflicting merge acceptance, replanning authority erasure, dependency-cycle acceptance, or hidden-cost erasure accepted as valid

probe described as deployed planner, runtime-scheduler, decomposition-quality, context-demand-prediction, route-quality, selected-tier-adequacy, scheduler-optimality, runtime-replanning, live-feedback, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Planning scheduler-state claim after deployed or externally reviewable scheduler traces, planner-harness evidence, dispatch and blocked-node traces under realistic dependencies, decomposition-quality measurement, context-demand prediction evidence, selected-tier or route-quality evaluation, scheduler-optimality or cost-quality comparison with visible residuals, runtime-replanning and live-feedback traces, residual accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent planner, scheduler, decomposition-quality, route-quality, selected-tier, live-feedback, benchmark, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable scheduler trace
no planner harness with real or externally replayed plan-node lifecycle states
no realistic dispatch and blocked-node trace
no route-quality or selected-tier adequacy evaluation
non_claims: does not promote Planning scheduler-state evidence above argument

does not prove deployed planner behavior, runtime scheduler behavior, decomposition quality, context-demand prediction, route quality, selected-tier adequacy, scheduler optimality, runtime replanning, live feedback handling, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Planning as a Control Layer chapter core claim
